author: @dont.ripfuller6587
text: Definitely want to use the former, it doesn't turn out the same when you use the ladder.

author: @calysagora3615
text: A nice replacement for sand is filling it with water and freezing it. Then just bend without kinks, melt and enjoy not having a mess to clean or fitting pressurized water or air to one end, to blow out the sand.

author: @mikereilly7629
text: That looks mighty familiar.i know how to use that,but the activated charcoal filter cloth doesn't work for the clay pot heater.

author: @KingJarreth
text:
Good morning from across the pond, sir!  As ever, thank you for all the information you test and share with us.
I'm looking into the helical bladed VAWT.  Do you believe there would be a significant trade off if I used a wider (and heavier) 2-blade helical configuration rather than a narrower and lighter winged helical VAWT?

author: @3dw3dw
text: Now if you fill it with water and solder the ends your bends will be perfect. You dont even need to f err eeze it or anything just get all the air out and seal the ends. Then there's no need to flush out sand or salt.

author: @stewartpalmer2456
text: LOL. Liquor control board is headed your way soon.

author: @paulwright8378
text: Do you know the difference between refraction an distillation

author: @milododds1
text: My father’s uncle was a machinist, after he was in retirement for many years he was a bit bored so he started working again making templates and things similar to your pipe to either form things or to test the tolerances or specifications of parts for some of the oil companies. Our world needs hands on people who can build and repair things. This is why I love this channel, very hands on and practical.

author: @CitizenJourno-1
text: you can also fill the pipe with water and freeze it then bend it around anything and it won't kink or damage the copper. try it it's pretty cool we did it to make an off-grid water heater using small pieces of wood in a jet stove to create the energy exchange. we wrapped the coils around the stack pipe. almost worked too well

author: @KJ-xt3yu
text: Or put water in the loop, connect it to a boiler, run the coil through a container that can deal with heat, and put some sand in it for the heat battery....have the steam/water feed into a piston of some sort and power a battery....

author: @travismoore7849
text: Could you use something like a transmission oil cooler for a methanol still?

text: Why don't you add fine sand Into the pipe to bend it?

author: @100musicplaylists3
text: A good tip is to fill it with water, freeze it and braze seal the ends before bending around the form to avoid squashing the tube into an oval shape.

replies.author: @DoNotPushHere
replies.text: I heard same thing with sand, you don't have waiting times

replies.author: @100musicplaylists3
replies.text: @DoNotPushHere yes, can be done with sand too.

replies.author: @charlesrichards5389
replies.text: Wouldn't the oval shape increase the efficiency of transferring heat?

replies.author: @100musicplaylists3
replies.text: @charlesrichards5389 i dont know but it would increase the chance of a blockage in the tube.

replies.author: @charlesrichards5389
replies.text: @100musicplaylists3 Yeah, depends a lot on what you're running through it. The passages in a car radiator are flat to maximize heat transfer, and they can run decades without plugging up.

author: @sween187
text: It's for heating water, put that around the chimney of your stove and out water through it (in at the bottom out at the top) the heating water will self circulate, now you can hook up a rad to it and bingo, your capturing that waste heat from your burner

replies.author: @KJ-xt3yu
replies.text: Heat battery, sand and coil, steam ouput to a piston/engine for power gen and store the excess in battery, while the heat buildup goes to the sand heat storage and you can put a fan + air ducting/pipe to pass the air through the heat battery
